name: tui-use
description: tui-use lets agents interact with programs that expect a human at the keyboard — REPLs, debuggers, TUI apps, and anything else bash can't reach.
readme_summary: tui-use enables AI agents to interact with terminal-based programs like REPLs, debuggers, and TUI applications, which are typically inaccessible to automated scripts. It provides a way to send keystrokes and read the screen output as plain text, bridging the gap between human interaction and agent control.
key_features: Smart Wait (debounce and text pattern matching)
Snapshot Model for explicit interaction loop
Highlights for identifying selected items in TUI apps
primary_use_case: Allowing AI agents to interact with and control terminal user interface (TUI) applications, REPLs, and debuggers that require human-like keyboard input and screen interpretation.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled puppeteer, representing the tui-use agent, subtly manipulating intricate strings connected to various terminal-based characters like a debugger, a REPL prompt, and a TUI application window. The puppeteer's hands are digital, glowing with soft code-like particles. The terminal characters are depicted as small, interactive dioramas, with the puppeteer's actions causing them to respond realistically. The background is a clean, abstract representation of a command line interface, with subtle data streams flowing around the scene. The overall mood is one of precise control and seamless interaction, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with a focus on clear visual storytelling and a modern, tech-inspired color palette.

name: musicat
description: A sleek desktop music player and tagger for offline music 🪕. With gapless playback, smart playlists, and a map view! Built with Svelte and Tauri
readme_summary: Musicat is a sleek, cross-platform desktop music player and tagger designed for managing local music libraries. It offers features like gapless playback, smart playlists, metadata editing, album art fetching, and unique visualizations such as waveform and map views, all built with Svelte and Tauri.
key_features: Supports MP3, FLAC, WAV, AAC, OGG
Download album art and origin country
primary_use_case: A desktop application for organizing, playing, and enriching local music libraries with advanced features and visualizations.
cover_image_prompt: A stylized, antique wooden music box sits open, revealing not gears, but a miniature, vibrant cityscape. From the music box, glowing streams of musical notes and album art flow outwards, connecting to various points on a stylized world map that hovers above. A small, sleek digital interface element, resembling a modern music player's controls, is subtly integrated into the side of the music box. The overall mood is warm and nostalgic, with a touch of digital magic. The scene is rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style, emphasizing the blend of classic music organization with modern digital enrichment and global discovery.

name: try
readme_summary: The 'try' repository provides a simple, zero-configuration Ruby script to manage and quickly navigate through numerous small experiment directories. It helps developers keep their exploratory code organized with features like fuzzy search, smart sorting, and auto-dating.
key_features: Smart sorting prioritizing recently used projects
Auto-dating for new experiment directories (e.g., YYYY-MM-DD-idea)
Option to use existing directories or clone repositories
primary_use_case: Organizing and quickly navigating through multiple small, experimental coding projects.
cover_image_prompt: A whimsical librarian in a vast, slightly chaotic library, surrounded by shelves overflowing with neatly labeled, glowing experiment journals. The librarian uses a magical magnifying glass that, when pointed at a shelf, instantly highlights the most relevant journal based on the user's query, bringing it forward. Small, subtle digital particles representing code float around the scene, and the library's main sign reads 'The Try Archives'. The overall mood is organized yet creative, with warm, inviting lighting. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a clean, friendly aesthetic.

name: Paper2Any
description: Turn paper/text/topic into editable research figures, technical route diagrams, and presentation slides.
readme_summary: Paper2Any is a Python-based tool designed to transform research papers, text, or topics into editable research figures, technical route diagrams, and presentation slides. It supports various input formats and leverages AI to automatically generate visual content, facilitating easier understanding and integration of complex information.
key_features: AI-Powered Generation of diagrams, plots, and slides
Image Model Playground for batch image generation
primary_use_case: To automate the creation of visual assets (diagrams, plots, slides) from research papers and technical documents, making complex information more accessible and editable.
cover_image_prompt: A diligent scholar in a cozy, well-lit study, holding a thick research paper. With a magical quill, they are effortlessly sketching intricate technical diagrams, vibrant experimental plots, and clean presentation slides that float out of the paper and onto a nearby digital canvas. Subtle glowing data streams connect the paper to the emerging visuals. The repository name 'Paper2Any' is subtly etched on the scholar's desk. The scene is rendered in a warm, inviting 3D isometric illustration style, emphasizing clarity and transformation.

name: AionUi
description: Free, local, open-source 24/7 Cowork app and OpenClaw for Gemini CLI, Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Qwen Code, Goose CLI, Auggie, and more | 🌟 Star if you like it!
readme_summary: AionUi is a free, open-source desktop application that functions as a Cowork platform, enabling AI agents to work alongside users on their computer. It allows AI to interact with files, execute multi-step tasks, and automate processes, with features like remote access and 24/7 scheduling.
key_features: AI agents work alongside you on your computer
Reads files, writes code, browses the web, automates tasks
Full file access for built-in agents
Autonomous multi-step task execution with user approval
Remote access via WebUI and messaging apps (Telegram, Lark, DingTalk, WeChat, WeCom)
Support for multiple AI agents simultaneously (Claude Code, Codex, Qwen Code, etc.)
primary_use_case: To provide a desktop environment where users can collaborate with multiple AI agents for tasks like coding, file management, and automation, with full transparency and control.
cover_image_prompt: A conceptual illustration of a bustling, futuristic co-working space where diverse AI agents, depicted as helpful robotic assistants and glowing orbs, are actively collaborating with human users. One AI is shown handing a complex blueprint to a human, another is sorting through a stream of digital data, and a third is remotely accessing a user's laptop via a holographic projection. Subtle UI elements and code snippets float in the background, indicating the software's functionality. The overall mood is productive and collaborative, with a clean, modern aesthetic. The repository name 'AionUi' is subtly integrated into a digital display. 3D isometric illustration style with vibrant, clean colors and soft lighting.
